label: no
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The problem is about a snail climbing a wall. The wall is 21 feet high. During the day, the snail climbs up 5 feet, but each night it slides back 3 feet. The question is asking how many days it takes the snail to reach the top. Hmm, first, I need to figure out the net progress the snail makes each day. If it goes up 5 feet during the day and slides back 3 feet at night, that means each full day (day and night) the snail makes a net gain of 5 - 3 = 2 feet. So, per day, it's 2 feet. But wait, this might not be the whole story. Because once the snail reaches or exceeds the top of the wall during the day, it won't slide back anymore. So the last day's climb
